pattern: Exchange every adjacent pair of nodes by relinking pointers, leaving a final odd node untouched.
hints: Process the list two nodes at a time; a lone trailing node (odd length) stays exactly where it is.
A dummy node before the head lets you treat the very first pair the same as every later pair, since the predecessor's next pointer must be redirected.
For each pair (first, second): point the predecessor at second, set first.next to second.next, set second.next to first, then advance the predecessor to first.

edge_inputs.description: single node has no partner to swap
odd length leaves the last node in place
duplicate values still swap by position

stress.description: long random list at the length cap

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def swapPairs(self, head):
        dummy = ListNode(0, head)
        prev = dummy
        while prev.next and prev.next.next:
            first = prev.next
            second = first.next
            first.next = second.next
            second.next = first
            prev.next = second
            prev = first
        return dummy.next
